dashboard.subtitleComplete: Your AI Navigator path is fully completed
dashboard.reviewTitle: Time for your Quarterly AI Health Review
dashboard.reviewDesc: Your last assessment is {weeks} weeks old. Regular reviews keep your AI progress on track.
dashboard.upgradeBannerDesc: PDF export, save results, versioning and all 7 tools.
dashboard.pathAllDoneDesc: All modules completed — compile your results into a PDF.

landing.heroDesc: 7 interactive tools for AI readiness, governance, use-case prioritization, and architecture — directly from the Enterprise AI Best Practice Guide.
landing.footerLegalNote: Legal pages (Imprint, Privacy Policy) are provided in German only, as required by German law.

ai.usageExceeded: Daily limit reached ({used}/{limit}) — deterministic result active
ai.failed: AI analysis unavailable — showing classic result
ai.confirmApplyDesc: The AI classification will be merged with your manual inputs. Compliance flags are preserved.

assessment.title: AI Readiness Assessment
assessment.subtitle: Measure maturity level
assessment.summaryDesc: Determine archetype and AI maturity across 6 dimensions

dimRec.data: Launch a data governance initiative, define master data model
dimRec.skills: Set up an AI upskilling program, appoint AI champions
dimRec.governance: Document AI policy and RACI within the next 4 weeks
dimRec.tech: Develop an API strategy for core systems, review cloud readiness
dimRec.strategy: Adopt the AI strategy at the next board meeting
dimRec.default: Secure executive sponsorship, launch an AI culture program

assessment.introDesc: Assess your company across 6 dimensions with {count} questions on a scale of 1–5 (L1 Initial to L5 Optimizing). Duration: {duration}.
assessment.introShortVersion: Short version (16 questions). With <pro>Pro</pro> you get the full 42-question assessment with an in-depth maturity model.
assessment.introDimHint: Click a dimension for weighting details

usecase.title: Use-Case Scoring
usecase.subtitle: Set priorities
usecase.desc: 5 criteria · portfolio matrix · prioritization by weighted score
usecase.summaryDesc: Evaluate AI use cases and prioritize by score
usecase.weightsHintP1: Each use case is evaluated on multiple criteria (e.g., strategic fit, data availability, compliance risk).
usecase.weightsHintP2: The weights determine which criteria are most important for your organization. This affects the ranking in the portfolio.
usecase.emptyDesc: Create your first use case to populate the matrix.
usecase.canvasHint: ◧ Linked Canvas will be considered in the Architecture Generator.
usecase.weightsError: Weights must add up to exactly 100 %.
usecase.matrixAriaLabel: Portfolio matrix with {count} use {count, plural, one {case} other {cases}}

canvas.title: AI Use-Case Canvas
canvas.subtitle: Develop use cases
canvas.desc: 8 fields · complete template · problem to next steps
canvas.summaryDesc: 8-field canvas: problem, solution, data, KPIs and next steps
canvas.contextAnalysisHintP1: As you fill in your canvas, the tool automatically analyses key terms and detects platforms, use case types, and compliance requirements.
canvas.contextAnalysisHintP2: These insights are used in other modules — for example, the architecture generator suggests suitable components when SAP or Azure is detected.
canvas.contextAnalysisHintP3: The analysis improves as the canvas becomes more complete ({count}/8 fields filled).
canvas.contextFillHint: Fill in more fields to get more precise recommendations ({count}/8 filled).
canvas.ctxUseSignals: Use detected signals automatically in other modules:
canvas.canvasTemplateDesc: Structured template for new AI initiatives — 8 fields, from problem to next steps.

governance.title: Governance Check
governance.subtitle: Responsible AI deployment
governance.desc: 6 gates · GDPR & EU AI Act · deployment approval
governance.summaryDesc: Review use case ethically, legally, and under EU AI Act
governance.useCaseSearchPlaceholder: Name of the AI use case to check
governance.infoTitle: What is the AI Governance Check?
governance.infoBody: This structured review process evaluates your AI use case against {totalSteps} ethical and legal gates — from the lawfulness of data processing to transparency and risk management. The result is not legal advice, but a well-founded guide for internal approval processes.
governance.infoTip: Tip: Select the answer that best describes the current state of your project — not the target state.
governance.actionFieldsHintTitle: What do the action areas mean?
governance.actionFieldsHintP1: These points come from gates you did not rate as “OK”.
governance.actionFieldsHintYellow: Yellow: needs improvement, but no immediate stop.
governance.actionFieldsHintRed: Red: critical gap that must be closed before go-live.
governance.actionFieldsHintFootnote: The recommendations are starting points — the final assessment rests with your team and, if necessary, legal counsel.

compliance.title: Compliance Center
compliance.subtitle: EU AI Act & GDPR
compliance.desc: EU AI Act · GDPR checklist · risk matrix · policy templates
compliance.summaryDesc: EU AI Act risk class check and GDPR obligations checklist
compliance.step1Hint: Select the EU AI Act risk class that applies to your primary AI use case. The matching obligation checklist will appear automatically.
compliance.prohibitedDesc: Systems in this class are prohibited in the EU under Art. 5 EU AI Act. There is no transitional law and no exceptions for commercial purposes. Consult a legal advisor before proceeding.
compliance.noObligations: No regulatory obligations under EU AI Act
compliance.noObligationsDesc: Systems with minimal risk are not subject to mandatory EU AI Act requirements. Recommended: voluntary Code of Practice and internal AI governance per ISO 42001.
compliance.noRiskClassSelected: Select a risk class above to see the matching obligation checklist.
compliance.watchlistNote: These entries are not compliance obligations. They indicate what could change. They will be added to the checklists after publication in the EU Official Journal (EUR-Lex).
compliance.bannerProhibitedMsg: Compliance check: EU AI Act classifies this use case type as prohibited. Implementation is not legally permissible.
compliance.bannerHighMsg: Compliance check: EU AI Act high risk detected. Review governance gates and data protection measures carefully.
compliance.bannerLimitedMsg: Compliance check: Limited risk under EU AI Act. Transparency obligations may apply — please verify.
compliance.watchlistDeadlineDays: {days, plural, one {⏱ # day left} other {⏱ # days left}}
compliance.watchlistDeadlineMonths: {months, plural, one {⏱ # month left} other {⏱ # months left}}
compliance.checklistHowP1: Click the status circle next to an item to cycle through three states:
compliance.checklistHowFail: ✗ Not fulfilled — reviewed, but a gap identified. These items appear as open points in the summary.
compliance.dsgvoHowTitle: How does the GDPR checklist work?
compliance.dsgvoHowP1: Click the circle next to an item to change its status:
compliance.dsgvoHowFail: ✗ Not fulfilled — gap identified, must be addressed
compliance.dsgvoHowSummary: Unfulfilled items appear as action items in the summary.
compliance.riskMatrixIntro: Position your AI use case by impact (in case of error) and probability of occurrence.
compliance.riskMatrixImpactHint: (What happens when the system is wrong?)
compliance.openItemsHintP1: All requirements you marked as “Not fulfilled” (✗).
compliance.openItemsHintP2: These gaps should be addressed before go-live — through technical measures, process adjustments, or documentation.
compliance.openItemsHintP3: Click the red status circle in the checklist to reset a status.
compliance.openItemsDesc: The following requirements were marked as “Not fulfilled” and must be addressed before go-live.
compliance.noOpenItems: ✓ No open points — all reviewed requirements are fulfilled.
compliance.extrasInfoBody: relevant to your organization. Activated regulations appear as fillable checklists — your progress is saved automatically.
compliance.activateHintP1: EU AI Act and GDPR apply to all AI projects. Depending on sector and company size, further requirements may apply:
compliance.activateHintISO42001: ISO 42001 is the international AI management standard — frequently required in enterprise procurement.
compliance.activateHintNIS2: NIS-2 has applied since October 2024 to critical infrastructure and digital services.
compliance.activateHintBAIT: BAIT is mandatory for banks and financial services firms under BaFin supervision.
compliance.activateHintLkSG: LkSG applies to companies with ≥ 1,000 employees — also relevant for AI projects with external data sourcing.
compliance.extrasEmptyState: Activate a regulation above to display the corresponding checklist.

architecture.title: Architecture Generator
architecture.subtitle: Define AI architecture
architecture.desc: Guided wizard · vendor-neutral reference architecture · RASIC & Compliance
architecture.summaryDesc: Generate vendor-neutral Enterprise AI reference architecture
architecture.prefillHint: {count} of 12 wizard steps pre-filled · All fields can be overwritten
architecture.catalogStale: ⚠ Catalog last updated {days} days ago — Admin Panel → Sync recommended
architecture.dsgvoWarningBody: The following recommended components are only conditionally GDPR-compliant. A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with the vendor as well as a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) may be required:
architecture.dsgvoConfirmButton: I confirm, the risk is acknowledged
architecture.dsgvoConfirmedMsg: Risk confirmed — architecture can be saved
architecture.dsgvoConfirmTitle: Please confirm the GDPR notice first
architecture.jouleTitle: Joule Use Cases for Your Industry
architecture.catalogSelectedDesc: These components were selected from the Technology Catalog based on your wizard inputs — evaluated by GDPR compliance, EU AI Act risk and hosting location.
architecture.catalogSelectedNote: Click on a component to see details like description, certifications and official website.
architecture.nextStepCanvasDesc: Translate your architecture into a concrete business case — with problem, solution, KPIs and stakeholders.
architecture.diagramLayersCount: {count} components in {layers} layers — available from Pro
architecture.catalogHintClick: Click on a component for details on GDPR status, EU AI Act classification and hosting.
architecture.costHintTitle: How should these estimates be read?
architecture.costHintBody: These indicative ranges are based on industry benchmarks and scale with your company size. They do not replace professional project cost planning.
architecture.costDisclaimer: Indicative values — depends on company size and project scope.
architecture.teamClickHint: Click a role for details, responsibilities and RACI mapping.
architecture.execLevelDisabledTooltip: Executive view is intentionally limited to L1
architecture.contextDetectedCanvas: Context detected: Canvas '{title}' · {count} of 8 wizard fields pre-filled
architecture.narrativeSaveFirst: Save first to activate AI assessment
architecture.narrativePlaceholder: AI assessment not yet generated — start with the ◆ button.
architecture.viewHintExec: Reduced to decision-relevant content — costs, benefits, risk, recommendation.
architecture.viewHintArchitect: Full view with all layers — detail level adjustable via L1–L3.
architecture.complianceHighlightHint: Components without compliance relevance are dimmed.
architecture.componentPickerHint: Deselect components to remove them from the architecture — conflicts and suggestions are detected automatically.
architecture.canvasScopeTitle: Which use cases should this architecture cover?
architecture.canvasScopeSubtitle: Select the Canvas use cases that should form the basis of this architecture. Default: all pre-selected.
architecture.canvasScopeConflict: Conflicting signals for {field} — no prefill set
architecture.canvasScopeEmpty: No canvases available — wizard starts without Canvas context.
architecture.techOptSummary: {components} components active · {suggestions} AI suggestions open · {conflicts} conflicts
architecture.rasicHint: Click a cell to cycle its value (R → A → S → I → C → empty). Changes are saved with the result.
architecture.eamWaiverPlaceholder: Why is this violation acceptable? (required, max. 200 chars)

roadmap.title: Roadmap Generator
roadmap.subtitle: Create implementation plan
roadmap.canvasHintTitle: How does the canvas influence the roadmap?
roadmap.canvasHintP1: The linked canvas comes from your highest-rated use case. It provides strategic context such as platform, compliance requirements, and stakeholder situation.
roadmap.canvasHintP2: The detected signals help you prioritize during phase planning. Switch to Canvas in the sidebar to edit the content.
roadmap.canvasNoSignals: No specific platform or compliance signals detected.
roadmap.topUseCasesLabel: Your Top Use Cases (from Use Case Scoring)
roadmap.archetypeFromAssessmentHint: Archetype based on your last assessment — you can change it below.

versions.compareNotAvailable: Comparison not available for this module yet.

selectionSidebar.title: My Architecture
selectionSidebar.selectedCount: {count, plural, one {# component selected} other {# components selected}}

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage profile and account
settings.deleteConfirm: This action cannot be undone. All your data will be deleted.
settings.pwRuleNumber: At least 1 number or special character
settings.billingDesc: Manage invoices, payment method and subscription via the Stripe portal.
settings.stripeNotLinked: Stripe customer account not linked yet. Please contact support.
settings.resetPathInfo: The dashboard path will show all steps as incomplete — your saved results remain fully intact.
settings.wizardResetConfirm: Reset guided path? Your existing results will be preserved.
settings.themeDesc: Customize the app color theme. Free for all users.
settings.archDiagramDesc: Diagram style, layout and color coding for the Architecture Generator.
settings.sharedLinksDesc: Overview of all active share links with expiry dates and access counts.
settings.deleteDesc: Permanently deletes your account and all associated data (Art. 17 GDPR). This action cannot be undone.

upgrade.subtitle: Unlock all features
upgrade.withdrawalWaiverLabel: I expressly agree that you begin performing the contract before the end of the withdrawal period. I acknowledge that I lose my right of withdrawal once performance has begun.
upgrade.withdrawalWaiverRequired: Please confirm the withdrawal waiver to proceed.

feedback.question: Was this tool helpful?
feedback.title: Feedback & Changelog
feedback.subtitlePage: Share your thoughts or discover new features.
feedback.placeholderBug: Describe the bug and how to reproduce it…
feedback.placeholderFeature: Which feature would help you? What's the context?
feedback.fileTooLarge: The file is too large (max. 5 MB).
feedback.successDesc: We will review your feedback and get back to you if needed.

share.createLinkDesc: Creates a publicly readable link. No login required for recipients.
share.linkCreated: Link created. Share by email or messenger.

ergebnisse.emptyAssessmentDesc: Start with the AI Readiness Assessment to determine your maturity level.
ergebnisse.emptyArchitectureDesc: Create your first Enterprise AI architecture in the Architecture Generator.
ergebnisse.emptyGovernanceDesc: Review your AI use cases against the 6 governance gates.
ergebnisse.emptyRoadmapDesc: Plan your AI implementation in three phases.
ergebnisse.emptyCanvasDesc: Structure your AI use case on the 8-field canvas.
ergebnisse.emptyComplianceDesc: Check your compliance with EU AI Act, GDPR and NIS-2.
ergebnisse.emptyUseCaseDesc: Evaluate and prioritize your AI use cases in the scoring matrix.
ergebnisse.primaryHintBody1: The result marked as Primary is used as the default context in other modules.
ergebnisse.primaryHintBody2Assessment: For example, the Architecture Generator automatically pulls the primary Assessment result as context — so you don't need to re-enter your maturity level.
ergebnisse.primaryHintBody2Canvas: A Canvas marked as Primary is automatically pulled into the Architecture Generator and other modules to provide context-sensitive recommendations.
ergebnisse.primaryHintBody2Other: Only one result per module type can be Primary at a time.
ergebnisse.compareHint2: Both entries selected — comparison shown below.
ergebnisse.primaryInfoAnchor: ★ Primary — Marks the entry used as the basis in other modules (Architecture Generator, Dashboard, PDF Export, Sharing). One entry per category can be set as primary.
ergebnisse.canvasIntro: The AI Strategy Canvas is your strategic foundation — Use Cases, Architectures and Roadmaps build on it.
ergebnisse.canvasHintBody1: The Canvas captures the strategic context of your AI project in 8 fields: Vision, Challenges, Stakeholders, Resources, Timeline, Risks, KPIs and Governance structure.
ergebnisse.canvasHintBody2: A Canvas marked as Primary is automatically transferred to the Architecture Generator and other modules to provide context-sensitive recommendations.
